mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits to equities through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, so 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive when the policy rate is in a sustained downtrend.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not positive on average during 3-month FEDFUNDS declines, or if the signal's rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly CPIAUCSL acceleration raises inflation surprises, lifts real-rate fears, and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3m annualized change rises above its 12m median
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-acceleration triggers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates labor-market deterioration and weaker future household income, which leads to lower S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise events are not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the event-study average return is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in PAYEMS and RSAFS signals broad employment and retail sales expansion, which supports earnings growth and higher S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-RSAFS expansion signal does not outperform either series alone, or if 60-day SPX returns are not positive in the top interaction quintile.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO combined with weaker HOUST indicates a broad production slowdown and softer cyclicals, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if joint weakness in industrial production and housing starts fails to predict negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is already high, further increases tighten the discount rate and hurt equity valuation, so S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 5 trading days after a rate hike.
failure_condition: Reject if rate-hike episodes above 4.5% are not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns, or if the post-trigger hit rate falls below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in RSAFS and PAYEMS after a soft patch signals accelerating consumption and payroll income, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the paired consumption-payroll recovery signal does not produce positive 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if returns are below baseline conditional on the signal.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle labor market, a flat PAYEMS reading combined with UNRATE at or below 4% reflects still-tight employment without deterioration, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE <= 4.0% and PAYEMS month-over-month change is nonnegative
failure_condition: Reject if the tight-labor trigger does not coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the conditional mean return is not higher than the unconditional mean by at least 0.5 percentage points.
